author: mjn
text: DECISION: Abacus will be a standalone tool (not embedded in beads) with its own SQLite DB, CLI, and MCP server. Beads integration via optional hooks — composable, not coupled. Rationale: different concerns (task tracking vs app modeling), different lifecycles, but same agent ecosystem.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Graph schema is API-first with an Action semantic layer. Auto-scan gives structure (Routes, Entities, Pages). Agents enrich with Actions that bridge Gherkin natural language to technical nodes. Actions are the cumulative knowledge that makes Gherkin-to-implementation possible.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Full BDD cycle — Gherkin → E2E test → implementation. Agent reads Gherkin, queries Abacus for matching Actions, generates step defs + E2E tests (red), then implements backend/frontend code (green). The graph is what makes this feasible without re-exploring the codebase every session.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: New Linear project 'Abacus' in the pcom-mike workspace. This is a distinct initiative serving E2E testing, feature implementation, onboarding, and general codebase understanding. Not scoped to the E2E Testing project alone.

author: mjn
text: INVESTIGATION: Current state — 382 beads knowledge entries, ALL from contest feature work. Zero entries about e2e, gherkin, playwright, auth flows, or general app structure. This gap is the motivating problem: reactive memories don't capture application shape proactively.

author: mjn
text: FACT: hourly/e2e/ already exists with Playwright + playwright-bdd, 1 feature file (auth/login.feature), 13 step defs, demo + e2e seed scripts. 45+ oRPC contracts, 30+ route files, CompanyOnboarding component exists. The structural data for auto-scan is rich.

author: mjn
text: INVESTIGATION: Target codebase (hourly) has 45+ oRPC contracts, 100+ Prisma models, 50+ React pages, existing Playwright + playwright-bdd setup with auth/login.feature (13 step defs). Rich dataset for MVP validation.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Match algorithm is tiered (Cucumber expressions → FTS5 fuzzy → LLM-assisted creation) and serves as a GROWTH mechanism. When no Action matches, the algorithm packages graph context as a suggestion for the agent to create a new Action. The graph learns from every feature.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Scanner architecture is pluggable from day 1. Scanner interface with registry, concrete implementations for oRPC, Prisma, React Router. Extensible to Express, GraphQL, etc.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: MCP server uses @modelcontextprotocol/server with stdio transport, Zod v4 for input schemas, better-sqlite3 sync API for instant responses. All logging to stderr.

author: mjn
text: FACT: CLI uses Commander.js + @commander-js/extra-typings for type-safe args. Binary names: abacus, ab. All commands support --json flag.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Switched from TypeScript to Go for core. Reasons: better compiler feedback for agents, single binary distribution, matches beads ecosystem (also Go/cobra), no CGo needed (modernc.org/sqlite). Scanners remain Node.js (BYOASTP).

author: mjn
text: DECISION: BYOASTP (Bring Your Own AST Parser) architecture. Scanners are standalone executables communicating via Scanner Protocol v1 (JSON over stdin/stdout). Abacus core has zero language-specific parsing. Any language can write a scanner.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Go MCP SDK uses mcp.AddTool() with struct-based input schemas (auto-generated via jsonschema tags). StdioTransport for Claude Code. MCP server is a cobra subcommand in the same binary: abacus mcp-server.

author: mjn
text: FACT: Official Go libraries exist for all needed components: MCP SDK (v1.4.0), cucumber-expressions-go, gherkin/go/v38, modernc.org/sqlite. Zero third-party gaps.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: TypeScript compiler messages are poor for agent feedback loops. When Sonnet agents hit TS compiler errors, messages are often cryptic ('Type X is not assignable to Y' with deeply nested generics) and lead to thrashing. Go compiler messages are precise, actionable, and include suggestions ('did you mean X?'). For agent-built tools, compiler feedback quality is a first-class design concern — choose languages where agents can self-correct from error output.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: BYOASTP (Bring Your Own AST Parser) — when a tool needs to parse multiple languages/frameworks, don't embed parsers. Instead, define a versioned JSON protocol over stdin/stdout and spawn parsers as subprocesses. Benefits: (1) core stays language-agnostic, (2) parsers can be written in whatever language parses that stack best, (3) fault isolation — crashing parser can't take down the host, (4) independent versioning, (5) community extensibility without forking. The protocol IS the interface. Trade-off: subprocess startup overhead (~100-500ms), but acceptable for scan-frequency operations.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Match failures as growth signals. When designing a lookup/match system, make the 'not found' path productive. Instead of returning empty, package the search context (what was queried, what related items exist) as a structured creation suggestion. The caller (agent or human) reviews and persists. This transforms every failure into a learning event. Applied in Abacus: Gherkin step with no matching Action → system returns related Routes/Entities/Pages + pre-filled Action template → agent creates Action → future queries succeed. The system gets smarter with each miss.

author: mjn
text: FACT: Go has a complete native ecosystem for building MCP-enabled CLI tools with graph storage: (1) github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk v1.4.0 — official MCP SDK with mcp.AddTool() and StdioTransport, (2) github.com/cucumber/cucumber-expressions-go — pattern matching with parameter extraction, (3) github.com/cucumber/gherkin/go/v38 — Gherkin file parsing, (4) modernc.org/sqlite — pure Go SQLite driver, no CGo needed, (5) github.com/spf13/cobra — CLI framework. Zero third-party gaps. All officially maintained.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: MCP server as cobra subcommand in same binary. Instead of shipping a separate MCP server executable, add 'abacus mcp-server' as a cobra subcommand. Claude Code config points to the same binary: {command: 'abacus', args: ['mcp-server']}. Benefits: single binary distribution, shared code with CLI, no separate install step, version consistency. The subcommand blocks on StdioTransport — all logging must go to stderr since stdout is the JSON-RPC protocol channel.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: When designing inter-process protocols (like Scanner Protocol v1), include: (1) version field for forward compatibility, (2) self-describing scanner metadata (id, name, version), (3) warnings array for partial failures (don't make it all-or-nothing), (4) stats block for observability (filesScanned, durationMs), (5) validation rules that the host checks before ingestion. Publish a JSON Schema file so implementers can validate offline. A good protocol is testable with echo: 'echo {json} | scanner-cmd' should work standalone.

author: mjn
text: FACT: hourly backend is an Express application. oRPC is used for contract definition and client SDK generation, but Express is the actual route infrastructure. Both scanners are essential and complementary: oRPC captures WHAT (typed contracts, schemas), Express captures HOW (middleware, auth guards, route mounting). Don't treat Express as legacy.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: Go module cache at /Users/mjn/go/pkg is read-only in the Claude Code sandbox. Must use GOMODCACHE=/tmp/.claude_scratch/gomodcache GONOSUMDB=* GONOSUMCHECK=* for all go commands.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Preserved existing .gitignore entries (.dolt/, *.db) from beads init and appended new Go-specific entries.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: modernc.org/sqlite supports FTS5 and unixepoch() out of the box — no special build tags needed. GOMODCACHE must be an absolute path.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Used content-sync FTS5 (content='nodes', content_rowid='rowid') with triggers rather than external-content manual sync, keeping FTS automatically in sync with the nodes table.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Pure-Go SQLite setup with modernc.org/sqlite: driver name is 'sqlite' (NOT 'sqlite3' which is mattn's CGO driver). Pragmas must be individual Exec() calls, not batched. Set WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, cache_size=-64000 (64MB), busy_timeout=5000. If any pragma fails, Close() the db before returning to prevent leaked connections.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: FTS5 content-sync trigger pattern for SQLite. The delete command syntax is counterintuitive: INSERT INTO table_fts(table_fts, rowid, cols) VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.cols). Table name appears as both target and column. UPDATE triggers must delete-then-insert (FTS5 has no native update). content_rowid='rowid' ties to SQLite's implicit rowid — breaks if table uses WITHOUT ROWID.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: SQLite graph adjacency list with enforced types. CHECK constraints on kind columns enforce enums at DB level. UNIQUE(src_id, dst_id, kind) prevents duplicate edges of same kind but allows multiple edge types between same nodes. ON DELETE CASCADE on edges auto-cleans when nodes are deleted. Properties as JSON TEXT DEFAULT '{}' — extensible without migrations, queryable via json_extract(). Adding new kinds requires schema migration (intentional — schema is the contract).

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Cobra CLI with ldflags version injection. Declare 'var Version = "dev"' (must be var, not const — linker can't override constants). Build with '-ldflags "-X full/package/path.Version="'. Full import path required — wrong path silently does nothing. Cobra's rootCmd.Version field + SetVersionTemplate gives --version flag for free. VERSION ?=  with fallback to 'dev'.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Used a --dir flag on init command to allow testing in temp directories. This keeps tests isolated without needing to chdir.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: cobra rootCmd is a package-level var, so tests reuse it across test functions. The --dir flag on init avoids test pollution by pointing at t.TempDir().

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Runner splits command string on whitespace (strings.Fields) rather than using shell parsing. This keeps the implementation simple and avoids shell injection. Complex commands can be wrapped in 'bash -c' by the caller.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: echo-scanner.sh test fixture uses heredoc with single-quoted delimiter ('EOF') to avoid variable expansion in the JSON output. The fixture consumes stdin but ignores it — just echoes hardcoded valid ScanOutput.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: ValidateOutput returns []string (error messages) rather than error, making it composable — callers can check len(errs) == 0 and format/join as needed. The runner joins them with semicolons for its error return.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: FTS5 content-sync join requires joining on rowid (n.rowid = fts.rowid), not on id. The content='nodes' and content_rowid='rowid' config in the FTS5 virtual table means FTS stores rowid references, not the text id primary key.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Used sql.NullString for nullable fields (source_file, scan_hash) to handle NULL values cleanly in scan operations, converting to *string pointers on the GraphNode struct.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Recursive CTE with UNION (not UNION ALL) for cycle-safe bidirectional graph traversal in SQLite. UNION inherently deduplicates visited nodes, preventing infinite loops in cyclic graphs without maintaining an explicit visited set. Query shape: WITH RECURSIVE connected(id, depth) AS (VALUES(?,0) UNION SELECT e.dst_id, c.depth+1 FROM edges e JOIN connected c ON e.src_id=c.id WHERE c.depth<? UNION SELECT e.src_id, c.depth+1 FROM edges e JOIN connected c ON e.dst_id=c.id WHERE c.depth<?). Two UNION branches traverse both edge directions.

author: mjn
text: FACT: validNodeKinds and validEdgeKinds are duplicated across db/types.go (Go constants) and scanner/validate.go (map[string]bool allowlists). Adding a new node or edge kind requires updating BOTH locations plus the SQLite CHECK constraint in schema.go. These are NOT derived from a single source of truth. This is the primary extensibility gotcha — forgetting one location causes silent validation failures.

author: mjn
text: FACT: RunAll merges scanner outputs by concatenation but does NOT run cross-scanner validation. Edges from scanner A referencing nodes from scanner B pass per-scanner validation but could fail at DB insert time. Also, two scanners emitting the same node ID results in last-write-wins via BulkUpsertNodes. No dedup detection exists at the merge layer. This is acceptable for MVP but needs SyncScanResults logic to handle properly.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Go repository testing with real SQLite via t.TempDir() — no mocking. setupTestDB creates a real in-memory or temp-file SQLite DB with full schema, registered for cleanup via t.Cleanup. This ensures DB behavior (triggers, constraints, FTS5) is tested faithfully. Combined with builder helpers (makeNode, validOutput) that provide default-valid test data where tests modify one field to trigger specific errors.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Subprocess plugin system with fail-open aggregation. RunAll captures per-scanner errors into MergedScanOutput.Errors and continues executing remaining scanners. Callers must check len(merged.Errors) > 0 after RunAll — a nil error return does NOT mean all scanners succeeded. This is the standard pattern for batch operations where partial results are more valuable than all-or-nothing.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: After JSON round-trip through SQLite (MarshalProperties/UnmarshalProperties), Go []string stored in properties becomes []interface{} when read back. Tests must use []any type assertion, not []string.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: ActionService.deleteEdgesFromByKind accesses repo.database directly (unexported field, same package) rather than adding a new method to GraphRepository. This keeps the repository API unchanged per file ownership rules.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: FTS5 BM25 ranks for small test datasets are very small negative numbers (e.g., -0.000003). A threshold of -10.0 incorrectly filters everything out. Default threshold should be 0 to accept all FTS5 results.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Used Tokenize() as shared function between FuzzyMatcher and SuggestionBuilder to avoid duplication. Exported it for testability.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Used regex-based .feature file parser (stepLineRe matching Given/When/Then/And/But) instead of gherkin-go library. Simpler, more reliable, no API compatibility issues across gherkin versions.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: cobra persistent flags (like --json on rootCmd) persist across Execute() calls in tests since they share the same rootCmd. Tests that depend on flag state must account for this.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: MCP go-sdk v1.4.0 AddTool generic function auto-generates input schema from Go struct tags. Handler signature is func(ctx, *CallToolRequest, InputStruct) (*CallToolResult, any, error). CallToolRequest is ServerRequest[*CallToolParamsRaw]. TextContent needs pointer receiver for Content interface.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Used a single queryNodesByKind helper method shared by query_routes, query_entities, query_pages to avoid duplication. Each handler just passes the appropriate NodeKind constant.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Stats handler uses GetNodesByKind with large limit rather than COUNT query since the repo API lacks a count method. This is acceptable for reasonable graph sizes.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: MCP Go SDK v1.4.0 tool registration uses generic AddTool[In, Out](server, tool, handler). Input struct tags: json for field names, jsonschema for descriptions. Handler returns (*CallToolResult, Out, error). Use &TextContent{Text: jsonStr} for response content. Tool names must be [a-zA-Z0-9_.-], max 128 chars. Import as gomcp to avoid package name collision with local mcp/ package.

author: mjn
text: FACT: JSON round-trip through SQLite (MarshalProperties → TEXT → UnmarshalProperties) converts Go []string to []interface{}. Code reading properties back must use type assertion to []any, not []string. This affects gherkin_patterns stored in Action properties.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Regex-based .feature file parser is preferred over gherkin-go library for CLI match/coverage commands. Pattern: stepLineRe matching Given/When/Then/And/But at line start. Simpler, no version compatibility issues, sufficient for step extraction. Full AST parsing only needed if we need Scenario/Feature metadata.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Shared query helper pattern for MCP tools. When multiple tools differ only by a NodeKind filter (query_routes, query_entities, query_pages), use a single private helper method (queryNodesByKind) that accepts the kind as a parameter. Each handler is a thin wrapper. Reduces duplication from N*50 lines to N*5 + 50.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: oRPC scanner uses ts-morph to find .route() PropertyAccessExpression calls, extracts object literal args, then walks the chain upward to find .input()/.output() identifiers. globSync from node:fs handles file discovery. 16 tests (14 unit + 2 integration).

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Prisma scanner uses regex-based parsing (no @prisma/internals dependency). Regex captures model/enum blocks, field attributes, @relation annotations. Only creates edges for owning side (has @relation(fields:...)). 30 tests.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Express scanner detects router.get/post/put/delete direct routes, router.route() chaining, and middleware extraction. Function calls like requireRole('admin') extract just the name. 18 tests.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: React Router scanner parses JSX <Route> elements with ts-morph (needs jsx: react-jsx in tsconfig). Handles nesting via parent walk for path composition, index routes, catch-all, and protection detection via ternary+Navigate pattern. 14 tests.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Scanner Template — all 4 scanners share identical skeleton: (1) index.ts reads ScanInput from stdin, calls parser, writes ScanOutput to stdout; (2) parser.ts has async function returning ScanOutput with try/catch per file → warnings accumulation; (3) types.ts duplicates protocol types (should be shared @abacus/scanner-protocol package); (4) Deterministic IDs: route:{METHOD}-{path}, entity:{ModelName}, page:{fullPath}; (5) Stats tracking: filesScanned, nodesFound, edgesFound, errors, durationMs. Template is identical enough to generate new scanners from.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Regex vs ts-morph for scanners — use regex for non-JS/TS inputs (Prisma schemas, YAML, SQL, .env) and ts-morph for actual TypeScript/JavaScript/JSX source code. The decision boundary is clear: if the input has a TS parser, use it. ts-morph requires skipAddingFilesFromTsConfig:true and noEmit:true to stay fast (parse-only, no type resolution). JSX parsing needs jsx:4 (ReactJSX) in compiler options.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: AST chain walking for fluent APIs — when parsing builder patterns like oc.route({}).input(S).output(S) or router.route('/path').get(h).put(h), the innermost call (.route()) is the deepest AST node. Walk UP via Parent→PropertyAccessExpression→Parent→CallExpression to find chained methods. Use a bounded loop (5-20 iterations) or walk until parent===null. Both oRPC and Express scanners use this technique.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: Scanner development checklist — (1) Always emit valid ScanOutput even on fatal errors (Prisma index.ts is the gold standard); (2) Use try/catch per file with warning accumulation, never let one bad file kill the scan; (3) Generate deterministic IDs from content ({kind}:{unique_key}); (4) Set source:'scan' and sourceFile (relative) on all nodes; (5) Return edges:[] and warnings:[] (not undefined) when empty; (6) Only emit edges for relationships your scanner can structurally detect (cross-scanner edges belong in a stitching phase); (7) Test protocol conformance by looping over all nodes asserting required fields.

author: mjn
text: FACT: Scanner code duplication opportunities — (1) resolveGlobs() is copy-pasted between oRPC and Express; (2) ScanOutput types are duplicated 4x across types.ts files; (3) stdin reading has two inconsistent patterns (sync readFileSync vs async chunked); (4) per-file try/catch→warning loop is identical in all ts-morph scanners. These should become a shared @abacus/scanner-protocol package. Prisma scanner is the cleanest reference (separates parsing from output building, most robust error handling, most complete test coverage).

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: React Router JSX scanner nuances — (1) Must handle both JsxSelfClosingElement (<Route .../>) and JsxElement (<Route>children</Route>) with different APIs; (2) Path composition for nested routes requires recursive parent walk (getParentRoutePath); (3) Protected route detection uses heuristic: walk up AST for ConditionalExpression inside JsxExpression where falsy branch contains Navigate; (4) Component extraction unwraps: JsxAttribute→JsxExpression→inner JsxSelfClosingElement→tag name. Most complex of the 4 scanners due to nesting and contextual detection.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: CLI query commands reuse the same GraphRepository/ActionService methods as the MCP server handlers. The CLI is a thin output-formatting layer over the same query API. Shared queryNodesCmd helper handles routes/entities/pages identically.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Shared queryNodesCmd() factory function creates cobra commands for routes/entities/pages. Each specific command file (routes.go, entities.go, pages.go) is ~10 lines calling this factory with NodeKind. Extra columns per kind: routes→METHOD, entities→FIELDS count, pages→PATH.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: Cobra persistent BoolVar flags leak between tests in the same package. Once --json is set true by one test, it stays true for subsequent tests using the same rootCmd. Fix: explicitly set --json=false in tests that expect human-readable output.

author: mjn
text: FACT: CLI package now has 33 tests total: 9 init, 8 match/coverage, 16 scan/query. All commands follow the pattern: open DB → create repo/services → query → format output (JSON or table). Shared queryNodesCmd factory generates 3 commands from a single function.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Cobra command factory for kind-filtered queries. When multiple CLI commands differ only by a filter parameter (NodeKind), create a factory function queryNodesCmd(kind, use, short) that returns a pre-configured *cobra.Command. Each concrete command file is ~10 lines calling the factory. Extra columns per kind are handled via a switch inside the factory. Applied: routes.go, entities.go, pages.go all use queryNodesCmd from nodes.go. Reduces N*120 lines to N*10 + 120.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: CLI-as-thin-wrapper-over-services. CLI commands and MCP server handlers call the same underlying services (GraphRepository, ActionService, MatchService, scanner.Runner) — they differ only in output formatting (table vs JSON-RPC). This ensures behavioral parity: if a query works in MCP, it works in CLI. Pattern: open DB → create repo/services → call same method → format for output target.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: Cobra persistent BoolVar flags leak between test invocations on the same rootCmd. Once --json is set true by one test's SetArgs+Execute, the bound variable stays true for subsequent tests. Cobra does NOT reset persistent flags to defaults between Execute() calls. Fix: explicitly pass --json=false in tests expecting human-readable output. This affects any persistent flag bound via BoolVar/StringVar.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Scan pipeline — config → runner → node ingest → edge ingest. The scan command: (1) resolves config.yaml path from --db flag path, (2) filters scanners by optional type arg, (3) runs scanner.Runner.RunAll() which spawns subprocesses, (4) converts ScanNode→GraphNode and bulk upserts, (5) ingests edges one-by-one with warn-on-failure (edges may reference cross-scanner nodes not yet ingested). Same pipeline in MCP scanHandler but with different output formatting.

author: mjn
text: FACT: Abacus CLI now has 8 commands beyond init/version: scan, routes, entities, pages, actions (with create subcommand), graph, stats, match, coverage. All support --json flag. 33 CLI tests total across 4 test files. The actions command uses parent+subcommand pattern (actionsCmd.AddCommand(actionsCreateCmd)) for extensibility (future: actions delete, actions update).

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Use mcp.NewInMemoryTransports() from Go MCP SDK for in-process MCP testing. Returns paired (serverTransport, clientTransport) via net.Pipe(). Server_test.go already tests handlers directly — the E2E test layers on transport to prove full JSON-RPC protocol. See .gomodcache/github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@v1.4.0/mcp/client_example_test.go for client usage.

author: mjn
text: FACT: Existing test helpers: setupTestDB (cli/match_test.go:15-48), setupTestDBWithNodes (cli/query_test.go:17-77), openTestDB/openAndInitTestDB (db/db_test.go:10-28), setupTestServer (mcp/server_test.go:15-42). Echo scanner fixture at testdata/scanners/echo-scanner.sh.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Skills will be delivered as a Claude Code plugin inside the abacus repo. Structure: plugin.json + skills/abacus-validate/SKILL.md + commands/. Anyone who clones abacus and installs the plugin gets the skills. Reference: ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/claude-plugins-official/plugins/example-plugin/ for plugin structure.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Claude Code plugin structure: .claude-plugin/plugin.json (name, description, author) + commands/ dir with markdown files. Commands use YAML frontmatter (description, argument-hint). Body is imperative instructions to the executing agent. Agents dir for autonomous agents with model/color frontmatter.

author: mjn
text: DECISION: Created 6 slash commands (abacus-scan, abacus-query, abacus-graph, abacus-coverage, abacus-new-scanner, abacus-validate) as commands/ markdown files. All use --json for programmatic output. abacus-validate doubles as aba-zca.20 implementation.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: Scanner config format in config.yaml uses map[string]ScannerConfig where keys are scanner IDs, not array entries. Skills reference the CLI commands directly via Bash tool.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: detectProject monorepo support — must scan subdirectory package.json files (backend/, frontend/) and Prisma paths (backend/prisma/schema.prisma). Root package.json in monorepos often only has client deps. oRPC detection broadened to any @orpc/* prefix.

author: mjn
text: INVESTIGATION: abacus init --force + scanner auto-detection required 3 iterations to work against real project (hourly). Issues: (1) old binary on PATH, (2) no --force flag to re-detect, (3) monorepo structure with deps split across subdirectory package.json files and Prisma in backend/prisma/. Each was only discoverable by testing against a real project, not unit tests.

author: mjn
text: LEARNED: Scanners must have sensible defaults when options are empty/undefined. Express defaults routeGlobs to ['**/*.ts','**/*.js'], oRPC defaults contractGlobs to ['**/*.ts'], React Router auto-detects route files from common locations (src/routes.tsx etc), Prisma falls back through backend/prisma/schema.prisma, server/prisma/schema.prisma, etc. Without defaults, scanners crash with 'globs is not iterable' when config has no options.

author: mjn
text: PATTERN: Scanner robustness pattern — always use optional chaining + nullish coalescing for options: const globs = options?.routeGlobs ?? ['**/*.ts']. Never trust that config.yaml options will be populated. Defense in depth: init populates defaults AND scanners handle missing options.
